Details: Series of seven bomb blasts on Mumbai Suburban Railway, pressure cooker bombs used, Western Line targeted, explosions during rush hour
Aftermath: High death toll, numerous injuries, Lashkar-e-Taiba suspected, attacks not linked to Srinagar grenade blasts

Details: Passenger train derails through sabotaged viaduct at Col des Nuages, 100 killed, two locomotives and 18 cars crash into ravine
Aftermath: Incident occurred during First Indochina War, frequent attacks by Viet Minh rebels, explosive charge detonated at viaduct, ravine plunge

Details: Bomb attack on Strasbourg–Paris train, deadliest terrorist attack in modern French history at the time, targeted No. 12 express train, derailed near Blacy, Marne, rails sabotaged with explosive device
Aftermath: Attack kept secret by French state, investigation findings, Communist Party leader demands inquiry

Details: Train hijacked by South Moluccans, 12-day standoff, 3 hostages killed, attackers demanded independent state, murders of hostages during standoff
Aftermath: Hijackers surrendered, convicted to 14-year sentences

Details: Train hijacked by 9 armed Moluccan nationalists, 50 hostages taken, hijacking lasted 20 days, ended with Dutch special forces raid
Aftermath: 2 hostages and 6 hijackers killed in raid, simultaneous hostage situation at elementary school in Bovensmilde

Details: Three bombings in Moscow, first on crowded train, second near KGB headquarters, third near Communist Party HQ
Aftermath: Three suspects executed, doubts on their guilt, Sakharov expressed concerns, no conclusive determination of perpetrators

Details: Khalistani militants stopped two trains near Ludhiana, opened fire on Hindu passengers, killing at least 80, attack occurred around 9:35 p.m.
Aftermath: Rescue team arrived at Badduwal station, survivors aided by local villagers, attacks followed national election violence and Rajiv Gandhi's assassination

Casualties: Mar 19: 14 killed, 49 wounded; Jul 3: 13 killed, 42 injured
Details: Mar 19: Suicide bombing at "20 January" station; Jul 3: Bomb detonated between "28 May" and "Ganjlik" stations
Aftermath: Temporary suspension of Baku Metro, State Investigation Commission formed, Armenian intelligence officers arrested
Suspects Charged: Mar 19: 11 indirect perpetrators charged, 2 sentenced to life, 9 to 15 years; Jul 3: Lone perpetrator sentenced to life

Details: Amtrak's Sunset Limited derailed by saboteurs, 2 locomotives and 8 cars derailed, notes found at the scene, rails shifted to cause derailment
Aftermath: Increased security measures by Amtrak, rerouting of the Sunset Limited, saboteurs never identified

Details: Series of attacks targeting public transport systems and a school, deadliest attack on July 25 in Saint-Michel station, gas bottle explosion, subsequent attacks in August, GIA claimed responsibility
Aftermath: Emergency response, casualties, confirmation of terrorist nature, deadliest attack in France since 1983, only deadly attack in GIA's 1995 campaign

Details: IED detonated on southbound tracks of Port-Royal RER station, 4 killed (2 French citizens, 1 Moroccan, 1 Canadian), no group claimed responsibility
Aftermath: "Vigipirate" security plan activated, police and army patrols, spot checks nationwide, Jean-Louis Bruguière and Jean-François Ricard in charge

Responsible Party: UNITA (National Union for the Total Independence of Angola)
Details: Train derailed by anti-tank mine, passengers attacked with small arms fire, 250 killed out of 500 passengers
Aftermath: United Nations Security Council condemned attack, UNITA claimed responsibility

Details: Bomb exploded in first class ladies' compartment, 10 killed, including 2 police constables, 70 injured, third in series of five bombings in eight months
Aftermath: Increased security measures, investigation ongoing, no suspects identified

Details: Suicide bombing on commuter train, explosion in Yessentuki, Chechen terrorists suspected, previous attacks on same line, Ibragim Israpilov convicted for related blast
Aftermath: No arrests or convictions for Yessentuki attack, Israpilov convicted for September blast, series of attacks in 2003

Details: Coordinated bombings on Madrid's Cercanías commuter train system, deadliest terrorist attack in Spain, Al-Qaeda in Iraq implicated
Aftermath: Controversy over government handling, miners who sold explosives arrested, political parties accused each other, nationwide protests

Details: Explosion on Shramjeevi Express train, carriage destroyed near Jaunpur, RDX explosive used, 13 killed, 50 injured
Aftermath: Train halted, emergency services extinguished fire, eyewitnesses reported suspects, authorities suspect Islamic extremists

Details: Fatal explosion on train between New Jalpaiguri and Haldibari, remote location, two passenger cars damaged, cause undetermined, suspected terrorism
Aftermath: Officials believe up to 66 injured, train 550 km from Kolkata, investigation ongoing

Details: Bombs detonated on Samjhauta Express, 70 killed, dozens injured, evidence of explosives found, intended to disrupt India-Pakistan relations
Aftermath: Both governments condemned attack, peace talks disrupted, suspects charged, mastermind killed, accused acquitted in 2019

Details: Bomb exploded under high-speed train between Moscow and Saint Petersburg, causing derailment, second bomb exploded during investigation
Aftermath: 27 deaths confirmed by 2 December, second bomb triggered by remote mobile phone, residents of Lykoshino first responders, field hospital set up, government confirmed terrorist attack

Location: West Midnapore district, West Bengal, India
Details: Train derailed due to missing track, struck by goods train, suspected sabotage by Maoist Naxalites, 13 carriages derailed, significant casualties
Aftermath: State police chief stated Naxalites claimed responsibility, heightened security, uncertainty over blast or derailment cause, investigation ongoing

Details: Suicide bombings at two Moscow Metro stations during morning rush hour, deadliest terrorist attack in Russian capital in six years, claimed by Caucasus Emirate leader Doku Umarov
Aftermath: Man who brought bombers arrested, Vagabov and four militants killed, series of attacks in Russia since 1994, Chechen militants influenced by Salafi beliefs

Details: Bomb exploded at Kastryčnickaja station, classified as terrorist attack, suspects arrested, motives unclear, perpetrators executed, government involvement debated
Aftermath: International condemnation, UN Security Council statement, political controversy, economic crisis, protests

Details: Two low-intensity bombs exploded on coaches S4 and S5 of the Bangalore–Guwahati Kaziranga Superfast Express, one female passenger killed, 14 others injured
Aftermath: Massive search operation, investigation ongoing, suspect detained found innocent, Special Investigation Team and NIA involved
Organizations Involved: Tamil Nadu Police, Special Investigation Team, National Investigation Agency

Details: Pipe bomb explosion near Bayrampaşa—Maltepe station, 5 people injured, terrorism-related incident, stub article
Aftermath: Investigation ongoing, no responsible party identified, article is a stub, help expand Wikipedia content

Details: 17-year-old Afghan refugee stabbed and injured five people on a train, shot dead by police after attacking them with an axe
Aftermath: Attacker shot dead by police, suspicion of Islamic State affiliation, five wounded including four Hong Kong tourists and one local German woman

Location: Jabri railway station, Shajapur district, Madhya Pradesh, India
Details: Terrorist attack on Bhopal–Ujjain Passenger train, first-ever strike in India by ISIS, 10 passengers injured
Aftermath: One suspect killed in Lucknow encounter, module self-radicalized, no financial support from ISIS, six arrested

Details: Explosive device in briefcase on metro, second device defused, ISIL plot in 2016, no public transport bombings since 2010, ISIL propaganda circulated, Putin visiting Saint Petersburg
Aftermath: Complex domestic terrorism threat, ISIL targeting Russia, sophisticated attack, Putin in Saint Petersburg

Details: Coordinated suicide attacks on London public transport during morning rush hour, four bombings on Underground trains and bus, 52 UK residents killed, over 700 injured
Aftermath: Deadliest terrorist incident in UK since 1988, first Islamist suicide attack in the country, caused by homemade explosive devices

Details: Time bomb hidden in suitcase detonated at Bologna Centrale railway station, roof collapse, main building destroyed, Ancona–Chiasso train hit, tourists present
Aftermath: Large number of casualties, insufficient emergency vehicles, public aid, reopened hospital departments

Details: Bombing on Brahmaputra Mail train, wrecked three carriages, derailed six more, at least 33 killed, bomb composition unknown, detonated by remote control device
Aftermath: Government blamed Bodo Security Force, official reports claimed 33 killed, some dispute the figure, Indian government desires to minimize impact of attack

Casualties: Feb 29: 28 British soldiers killed, 35 wounded; Mar 31: 40 civilians killed, 60 wounded
Details: Feb 29: Train mined north of Rehovot, bombs detonated from orange grove; Mar 31: Train mined near Binyamina, all casualties civilians, Lehi claimed responsibility
Aftermath: Series of train bombings during 1948 Palestine war, retaliation for Ben Yehuda Street Bombing, attacks began in 1947

Details: Premature detonation of IRA incendiary bomb on Ballymena to Belfast passenger train, 3 killed, 5 injured, statement issued by IRA acknowledging responsibility
Aftermath: Train engulfed in flames, survivors evacuated, damaged carriages examined, one in service until 2006, other until 2012

Details: Male suicide bomber detonated explosives near Avtozavodskaya station, injuries included broken bones and smoke inhalation, Chechen separatists blamed, previously unknown group claimed responsibility
Aftermath: Chechen rebel leaders denied involvement, President Putin blamed separatists, group claimed attack to mark anniversary of Novye Aldi massacre
Suspects Charged: Tambiy Khudiyev, Maksim Panaryin, Murad Shavayev

Location: San Benedetto Val di Sambro tunnel, Italy
Details: Bomb attack on Italicus Express night train, 12 killed, 48 wounded, bomb placed in fifth passenger car, explosion at 01:23
Aftermath: Train reached end of tunnel, Aldo Moro had been on train day before, Ordine Nero claimed responsibility
Suspects Charged: Mario Tuti's subversive commando unit: Piero Malentacchi, Luciano Franci, Margherita Luddi
Organizations Involved: Ordine Nero, Fronte Nazionale Rivoluzionario, Ordine Nuovo

Details: Bomb placed on bicycle near Vile Parle railway station, explosion killed one, injured 28, coincided with Prime Minister Vajpayee's visit
Aftermath: Second in series of five bombings in Mumbai, Prime Minister's visit unaffected, no responsible party identified

Responsible Party: Suspected Student's Islamic Movement of India
Details: Shramjeevi Express derails after hitting broken rail, 12 killed, 80 injured, fishplates removed from rails, suspected terrorist attack
Aftermath: Prompt rescue efforts, injured taken to local hospitals, investigation points to terrorist sabotage

Details: Derailment and crash of Kacheguda - Bengaluru City Superfast Express, 20 killed, 80 injured, one rail track deliberately severed, no terrorist organization claimed responsibility
Aftermath: Emergency services responded, 30 hospitalized, line cleared for service, sabotage confirmed, no motive established

Responsible Party: Japanese Communist Party, Japan National Railway Union
Details: Tōhoku Main Line passenger train derailed and overturned, sabotage suspected, bolts and nuts on track joints loosened
Aftermath: 20 arrested, 17 convicted in 1953, all acquitted on appeal, case closed in 1970, archive files made public in 2009
Suspects Charged: Ten workers from Matsukawa plant, ten workers from Japan National Railway
Organizations Involved: Japanese Communist Party, Japan National Railway Union

Details: Unmanned train incident at Mitaka Station, Chūō Line, 63 series train, 6 killed, 20 injured, mysterious circumstances
Aftermath: Indictment of ten for train sabotage, conductor charged, court ruling in 1955, Takeuchi sentenced to death

Location: Parsons Green Underground station, London, England
Casualties: 30 treated in hospital, mostly for burn injuries
Details: Explosion on District line train, botched "bucket bomb" with TATP, main suspect arrested, several addresses raided, classified as jihadist terrorism
Aftermath: Main suspect arrested, foster home raided, suspect's background revealed, classified as jihadist terrorism

Details: Derailment of Howrah Rajdhani Express on Dhave River bridge, reportedly due to sabotage by Naxalites, 130 killed, 150 injured
Aftermath: Rescue efforts hampered by poor roads, 125 people rescued, bodies recovered from river, death toll potentially higher

Details: Series of bombings on Rizal Day, 5 locations bombed almost simultaneously, bombs made of black powder and ammonium nitrate-based explosives, components sourced from Talisay, Cebu
Aftermath: Case remained unsolved for almost three years, suspect Saifullah Yunos arrested in 2003, confessed to involvement, charged with multiple murders
Organizations Involved: Jemaah Islamiyah, Moro Islamic Liberation Front, Moro National Liberation Front

Casualties: 14 killed, 50 severely injured, nearly 1,000 with temporary vision problems
Details: Coordinated sarin attacks on three Tokyo Metro lines during rush hour, directed against trains passing through Kasumigaseki and Nagatachō, led by Shoko Asahara
Aftermath: Arrest of over 200 cult members, including Asahara, 13 senior Aum management sentenced to death, deadliest terrorist incident in Japan
Suspects Charged: Shoko Asahara, other senior Aum members
trigger: Sarin gas attack on the Tokyo subway

Details: Time bomb exploded inside Tarnów train station, 20 killed, 35 wounded, attack carried out by German saboteur Anton Guzy
Aftermath: Attack occurred two days before the start of World War II, saboteur identified as Anton Guzy, 20 people killed instantly

Details: Bomb detonated on Naples to Milan express train, 16 killed, 266 wounded, motive to distract security forces from investigating Mafia, Mafia boss Giuseppe Calò convicted
Aftermath: Mafia boss Salvatore Riina indicted for ordering bombing, Calò convicted, attack to distract from Mafia investigation
